name: American Jewelry & Loan - Pontiac
description_short: Detroit-area pawn chain operating since 1978, known from TV's Hardcore Pawn. Offers collateral loans, retail buying, and selling of jewelry, electronics, tools, and luxury goods.
description_long:
American Jewelry and Loan is a Michigan institution founded in 1978, making it one of the longest-running pawn operations in the Detroit metro area. The company gained widespread national recognition as the setting for truTV's reality series Hardcore Pawn, which ran for multiple seasons and featured the shop's day-to-day transactions. Despite its television fame, the company operates as a full-service pawn shop across four Michigan locations: Detroit (the flagship), Hazel Park, Pontiac, and Southgate.

The Pontiac location, like all American Jewelry and Loan branches, offers three core services: pawn loans, retail buying, and direct selling. Pawn loans are collateral-based short-term advances secured against items including jewelry, electronics, tools, and other valuables. The retail buying side features GIA Certified Diamond jewelry, luxury handbags, fine jewelry, timepieces, electronics, and tools — all sold at a fraction of retail price. Customers can also sell their own valuables directly to the shop's appraisers at what the company describes as fair market value.

What distinguishes American Jewelry and Loan from most regional pawn shops is its scale, brand recognition, and breadth of high-value inventory. The GIA certification mention signals that at least some diamond inventory has been independently graded, which provides more transparency than unverified pawn merchandise. The chain's television history has made it one of the most recognizable pawn brands in the country, and its 45-plus years of continuous operation suggests sustained community presence in a competitive industry.

As with any pawn shop, consumers should go in with realistic expectations. Pawn loan interest rates and fees are typically high compared to other lending options, and loan amounts are based on the shop's assessed resale value — not sentimental or retail value — of the collateral. The Pontiac location's specific hours, staff, and inventory are not detailed separately on the website, which consolidates all locations under a single web presence. This is a solid option for fast cash against physical assets or discounted retail purchases, but it is not suitable for large loan amounts or those seeking traditional credit products.
pros: Over 45 years in operation — founded in 1978, one of the oldest pawn chains in Detroit
Four Michigan locations (Detroit, Hazel Park, Pontiac, Southgate) for regional convenience
GIA Certified Diamond jewelry available for purchase — independent grading adds transparency
Three-sided marketplace: pawn loans, retail buying, and direct selling all under one roof
Nationally recognized brand from Hardcore Pawn TV series — extensive public track record
Accepts wide variety of collateral: jewelry, electronics, tools, luxury handbags, timepieces
Appointment booking available — reduces wait time for transactions
cons: Pawn loan terms (interest rates, fees, redemption periods) are not disclosed on the website
Closed Sundays — limited weekend access compared to shops with 7-day hours
All four locations share one phone number and email, making it harder to reach the Pontiac branch directly
Loan amounts based on resale value assessed by the shop, typically well below retail or sentimental value
No online loan application or remote appraisal — in-person visit required for all transactions
best_for: Detroit-area residents needing fast cash against jewelry, electronics, or tools
Bargain shoppers seeking discounted luxury goods, certified diamond jewelry, or timepieces
Sellers looking to convert used electronics or jewelry into immediate cash
Consumers who need a short-term collateral loan and have no credit or poor credit history
diagnosis: American Jewelry and Loan - Pontiac is best for Michigan residents who need quick cash against physical collateral or want to buy discounted luxury and jewelry items without retail markups. The main caveat is that pawn loan rates are not disclosed online and are typically high — this is a last-resort cash option, not a low-cost borrowing solution.
services: GIA Certified Diamond jewelry retail sales
Direct item purchasing (sell your valuables to the shop)
